understand that, like, since I've been involved in that team a few times, it's quite a tough task.

We are using Temporal to serve the back end for the most part, along with, or to serve our rails.

That stack works like, you know, Atlas as a front end allows me to manipulate pipelines a little bit using just YAML, you know, standard scripting.
It's too crazy.
And then on the back end, of course, it interacts with the pipelines that are created, shipped, and served via Temporal and then Flow Studio, which is our back end.
And the Flow Studio pipelines allow us to basically just create automations by linking various workflows together into pipelines.
And those automations could be anything for...
From, you know, content generation to research to fact-checking, or in some cases, publishing, right?
Or image generation, a variety of other things.

We work fast.
We work nimbly.

It should, and it will, and hopefully should apply to a bunch of different modes of work and different applications.
So it could be like sales funnel enrichment.
It could be, you know, production pipelines.
There are a lot of different applications for what we're building.

It's basically taking and organizing the messy middle of work and building automation in between to handle a lot of the steps that we would normally kind of fall into the cracks or fall into the institutional knowledge of a single person.
And then when that person leaves, or maybe they change job positions, now they have to transmit a lot of that learning to someone else.
And so there's just not a lot of work done to organize the work that is being done and the processes by which we do it.
And that's what we're essentially building at a granular level.

Right now, the biggest application for it is content creation, content strategy, growth strategy, building things like link magnets or dashboards, directories, programmatic rollouts of things like, as an example, for one client, Vappy, we did a programmatic rollout where we used Vappy's API to generate basically hundreds or eventually thousands of examples of how their API could be leveraged to create a voice agent that could answer the phonic and answering service for

So Vertical X, and Vertical X is like any one of thousands of verticals, but then we were able to programmatically generate them so that it served a live demo to any particular person having to walk in looking for something for their specific ICP, you know.

Cool, we've got an answering service just for you.
You know, are you in concrete?
Cool, we've got one for you.

Blog posts with all the proper fields, metadata, tagging, et cetera.

You know, building the ability for them to say, this is good, I release you into the wild, and for the rest of it to happen programmatically.

And so publishing those manually is crazy.
You know, it's out of the question.
And really long-term, definitely a nice hack when you have to, but not sustainable.
And so that's kind of what we're looking for there.

think it comes down to WordPress, Sanity, and...

worst case scenario, which is abnormal security in our case, is we have to do a manual interaction.

know, you're here.

yeah.

As soon as you get the picture, have the GPT process it so you don't have to wait until the end to get it processed.
